LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC, EUROMOTORS, INC. DBA MERCEDES-BENZ OF SAN FRANCISCO, BERBERIAN BROS., INC. DBA BERBERIAN EUROPEAN MOTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS TO THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY AS AGAINST MBUSA, WHICH IS AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM NOTWITHSTANDING BEING INCLUDED WITHIN THE SAME ENUMERATED CAUSE OF ACTION AS THE EXPRESS WARRANTY CLAIM. THE CLAIM IS TIME BARRED. THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED UPON THE DATE OF TENDER (APRIL 30, 2006), AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE SUIT WITHIN 4 YEARS THEREAFTER. SEE CAL. COMM. CODE SEC. 2725. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO THE BREACH OF EXPRESS WARRANTY CAUSE OF ACTION AS AGAINST MBUSA. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED AND/OR WHETHER THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WAS TOLLED OR ESTOPPEL APPLIES IN LIGHT OF CONTESTED ISSUES CONCERNING THE CONTENT OF AND REASONABLE INFERENCES TO BE DRAWN FROM COMMUNICATIONS IN OCTOBER OF 2009. THE REMAINING ISSUES IN THIS MOTION ARE DENIED AS MOOT BASED ON THE DISMISSALS FILED ON OCTOBER 30, 2014. THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY EMAIL. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302/PJB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Or In The Alternative, Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC, EUROMOTORS, INC. DBA MERCEDES-BENZ OF SAN FRANCISCO, BERBERIAN BROS., INC. DBA BERBERIAN EUROPEAN MOTORS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Or, In The Alternative, Summary Judgment. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants the motion as to the claim for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability as against MBUSA, which is an independent claim notwithstanding being included within the same enumerated cause of action as the express warranty claim. The claim is time barred. The cause of action accrued upon the date of tender (April 30, 2006), and plaintiff failed to file suit within 4 years thereafter. See Cal. Comm. Code sec. 2725. The Court denies the motion as to the breach of express warranty cause of action as against MBUSA. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding when the cause of action accrued and/or whether the statute of limitations was tolled or estoppel applies in light of contested issues concerning the content of and reasonable inferences to be drawn from communications in October of 2009. The remaining issues in this motion are denied as moot based on the dismissals filed on October 30, 2014. The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
